heading: Definitions and special rules
label: 150 Definitions and special rules

children.heading: Bond
children.label: 1 Bond
children.text: The term “bond” includes any obligation.

children.heading: Governmental unit not to include Federal Government
children.label: 2 Governmental unit not to include Federal Government
children.text: The term “governmental unit” does not include the United States or any agency or instrumentality thereof.

children.heading: Net proceeds
children.label: 3 Net proceeds
children.text: The term “net proceeds” means, with respect to any issue, the proceeds of such issue reduced by amounts in a reasonably required reserve or replacement fund.

children.heading: 501(c)(3) organization
children.label: 4 501(c)(3) organization
children.text: The term “501(c)(3) organization” means any organization described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from tax under section 501(a).

children.heading: Ownership of property
children.label: 5 Ownership of property
children.text: Property shall be treated as owned by a governmental unit if it is owned on behalf of such unit.

children.heading: Tax-exempt bond
children.label: 6 Tax-exempt bond
children.text: The term “tax-exempt” means, with respect to any bond (or issue), that the interest on such bond (or on the bonds issued as part of such issue) is excluded from gross income.

children.heading: Change in use of facilities financed with tax-exempt private activity bonds
children.label: b Change in use of facilities financed with tax-exempt private activity bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any residence with respect to which financing is provided from the proceeds of a tax-exempt qualified mortgage bond or qualified veterans’ mortgage bond, if there is a continuous period of at least 1 year during which such residence is not the principal residence of at least 1 of the mortgagors who received such financing, then no deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for interest on such financing which accrues on or after the date such period began and before the date such residence is again the principal residence of at least 1 of the mortgagors who received such financing.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: B Exception
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the extent the Secretary determines that its application would result in undue hardship and that the failure to meet the requirements of subparagraph (A) resulted from circumstances beyond the mortgagor’s control.

children.label: A
children.text: with respect to which financing is provided from the proceeds of any private activity bond which, when issued, purported to be a tax-exempt bond described in paragraph (7) of section 142(a), and

children.label: B
children.text: which does not meet the requirements of section 142(d),

children.label: i
children.text: is used in a trade or business of any person other than a 501(c)(3) organization or a governmental unit, but

children.label: ii
children.text: continues to be owned by a 501(c)(3) organization,

children.heading: Denial of deduction for interest
children.label: B Denial of deduction for interest
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for interest on financing described in subparagraph (A) which accrues during the period beginning on the date such facility is used as described in subparagraph (A)(i) and ending on the date such facility is not so used.

children.heading: Certain exempt facility bonds and small issue bonds
children.label: 4 Certain exempt facility bonds and small issue bonds

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any facility with respect to which financing is provided from the proceeds of any private activity bond to which this paragraph applies, if such facility is not used for a purpose for which a tax-exempt bond could be issued on the date of such issue, no deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for interest on such financing which accrues during the period beginning on the date such facility is not so used and ending on the date such facility is so used.

children.heading: Bonds to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Bonds to which paragraph applies
children.text: This paragraph applies to any private activity bond which, when issued, purported to be a tax-exempt exempt facility bond described in a paragraph (other than paragraph (7)) of section 142(a) or a qualified small issue bond.

children.heading: Facilities required to be owned by governmental units or 501(c)(3) organizations
children.label: 5 Facilities required to be owned by governmental units or 501(c)(3) organizations
A
children.text: financing is provided with respect to any facility from the proceeds of any private activity bond which, when issued, purported to be a tax-exempt bond,

children.label: B
children.text: such facility is required to be owned by a governmental unit or a 501(c)(3) organization as a condition of such tax exemption, and

children.label: C
children.text: such facility is not so owned,

children.heading: Small issue bonds which exceed capital expenditure limitation
children.label: 6 Small issue bonds which exceed capital expenditure limitation
children.text: In the case of any financing provided from the proceeds of any bond which, when issued, purported to be a qualified small issue bond, no deduction shall be allowed under this chapter for interest on such financing which accrues during the period such bond is not a qualified small issue bond.

children.heading: Exception and special rules for purposes of subsection (b)
children.label: c Exception and special rules for purposes of subsection (b)

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 1 Exception
children.text: Any use with respect to facilities financed with proceeds of an issue which are not required to be used for the exempt purpose of such issue shall not be taken into account.

children.heading: Treatment of amounts other than interest
children.label: 2 Treatment of amounts other than interest
children.text: If the amounts payable for the use of a facility are not interest, subsection (b) shall apply to such amounts as if they were interest but only to the extent such amounts for any period do not exceed the amount of interest accrued on the bond financing for such period.

children.heading: Use of portion of facility
children.label: 3 Use of portion of facility
children.text: In the case of any person which uses only a portion of the facility, only the interest accruing on the financing allocable to such portion shall be taken into account by such person.

children.heading: Cessation with respect to portion of facility
children.label: 4 Cessation with respect to portion of facility
children.text: In the case of any facility where part but not all of the facility is not used for an exempt purpose, only the interest accruing on the financing allocable to such part shall be taken into account.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 5 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this subsection and subsection (b).

children.heading: Treatment as State or local bond
children.label: 1 Treatment as State or local bond
children.text: A qualified scholarship funding bond shall be treated as a State or local bond.

children.label: A
children.text: is a corporation not for profit established and operated exclusively for the purpose of acquiring student loan notes incurred under the Higher Education Act of 1965, and

children.label: B
children.text: is organized at the request of the State or 1 or more political subdivisions thereof or is requested to exercise such power by 1 or more political subdivisions and required by its corporate charter and bylaws, or required by State law, to devote any income (after payment of expenses, debt service, and the creation of reserves for the same) to the purchase of additional student loan notes or to pay over any income to the United States.

children.heading: Election to cease status as qualified scholarship funding corporation
children.label: 3 Election to cease status as qualified scholarship funding corporation

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any qualified scholarship funding bond, and qualified student loan bond, outstanding on the date of the issuer’s election under this paragraph (and any bond (or series of bonds) issued to refund such a bond) shall not fail to be a tax-exempt bond solely because the issuer ceases to be described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (2) if the issuer meets the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of this paragraph.

children.heading: Assets and liabilities of issuer transferred to taxable subsidiary
children.label: B Assets and liabilities of issuer transferred to taxable subsidiary
i
children.text: all of the student loan notes of the issuer and other assets pledged to secure the repayment of qualified scholarship funding bond indebtedness of the issuer are transferred to another corporation within a reasonable period after the election is made under this paragraph;

children.label: ii
children.text: such transferee corporation assumes or otherwise provides for the payment of all of the qualified scholarship funding bond indebtedness of the issuer within a reasonable period after the election is made under this paragraph;

children.label: iii
children.text: to the extent permitted by law, such transferee corporation assumes all of the responsibilities, and succeeds to all of the rights, of the issuer under the issuer’s agreements with the Secretary of Education in respect of student loans;

children.label: iv
children.text: immediately after such transfer, the issuer, together with any other issuer which has made an election under this paragraph in respect of such transferee, hold all of the senior stock in such transferee corporation; and

children.label: v
children.text: such transferee corporation is not exempt from tax under this chapter.

children.heading: Issuer to operate as independent organization described in section 501(c)(3)
children.label: C Issuer to operate as independent organization described in section 501(c)(3)
i
children.text: the issuer is described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from tax under section 501(a);

children.label: ii
children.text: the issuer no longer is described in subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (2); and

children.label: iii
children.text: at least 80 percent of the members of the board of directors of the issuer are independent members.

children.label: i
children.text: which participates pro rata and fully in the equity value of the corporation with all other common stock of the corporation but which has the right to payment of liquidation proceeds prior to payment of liquidation proceeds in respect of other common stock of the corporation;

children.label: I
children.text: the fair market value of such stock on the date of liquidation or redemption (whichever is applicable); or

children.label: II
children.text: the fair market value of all assets transferred in exchange for such stock and reduced by the amount of all liabilities of the corporation which has made an election under this paragraph assumed by the transferee corporation in such transfer;

children.label: iii
children.text: the holder of which has the right to require the transferee corporation to redeem on a date that is not later than 10 years after the date on which an election under this paragraph was made and pursuant to such election such stock was issued; and

children.label: iv
children.text: in respect of which, during the time such stock is outstanding, there is not outstanding any equity interest in the corporation having any liquidation, redemption or dividend rights in the corporation which are superior to those of such stock.

children.label: i
children.text: for services performed in connection with such transferee corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: for services as a member of the board of directors or as an officer of such transferee corporation.

children.heading: Coordination with certain private foundation taxes
children.label: F Coordination with certain private foundation taxes
i
children.text: the last day of the last taxable year for which more than 50 percent of the gross income of such transferee corporation is derived from, or more than 50 percent of the assets (by value) of such transferee corporation consists of, student loan notes incurred under the Higher Education Act of 1965; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the last day of the taxable year of the issuer during which occurs the date which is 10 years after the date on which the election under this paragraph is made.

children.heading: Election
children.label: G Election
children.text: An election under this paragraph may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Bonds of certain volunteer fire departments
children.label: e Bonds of certain volunteer fire departments
A
children.text: such department is a qualified volunteer fire department with respect to an area within the jurisdiction of such political subdivision, and

children.label: B
children.text: such bond is issued as part of an issue 95 percent or more of the net proceeds of which are to be used for the acquisition, construction, reconstruction, or improvement of a firehouse (including land which is functionally related and subordinate thereto) or firetruck used or to be used by such department.

children.label: A
children.text: which is organized and operated to provide firefighting or emergency medical services for persons in an area (within the jurisdiction of such political subdivision) which is not provided with any other firefighting services, and

children.label: B
children.text: which is required (by written agreement) by the political subdivision to furnish firefighting services in such area.

children.heading: Treatment as private activity bonds only for certain purposes
children.label: 3 Treatment as private activity bonds only for certain purposes
children.text: Bonds which are part of an issue which meets the requirements of paragraph (1) shall not be treated as private activity bonds except for purposes of sections 147(f) and 149(d).
